body:
Closes #1408

Preview: https://deploy-preview-1741--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/navigation-menu

To do:

- [x] Nested menus
- [x] Tailwind demos
- [x] Tests/bugfixes
- [x] Full interruptibility of transitions

| --- _To edit notification comments on pull requests, go to your [Netlify project configuration](https://app.netlify.com/projects/base-ui/configuration/notifications#deploy-notifications)._

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/33f49757-3ed1-47db-8a81-5ad6a6362393

I noticed the scrollbar on the 2nd item flashed when hovering from trigger 1 > trigger 2 (at ~4s)

@mj12albert  I see this issue happens with `Always` scrollbars. I will try to see if I can do something else to fix this problem, but using our `ScrollArea` component can avoid it since it uses overlay scrollbars that can be hidden while transitioning the scrollable content
I decided to remove the scrollability of the popups from the demos by default and instead add a section of how to handle it.
I'd intuitively expect the <kbd>DownArrow</kbd> to open the menu, as in Menubar. Also, when the menu is open, the focus should be placed within the popup.

@michaldudak I think the `<nav>` role has a few different focus semantics with composite keys + tabbing, in this case I did what Radix does, but there's definitely some debate about how this should be handled. 

cc: @colmtuite 

- https://www.makethingsaccessible.com/guides/site-navigation-is-not-an-aria-menu/
- https://adrianroselli.com/2017/10/dont-use-aria-menu-roles-for-site-nav.html

Radix works slightly better, as when the popup is open, it allows focusing its contents with the <kbd>DownArrow</kbd> while our solution focuses the next nav menu item.

I like interactions in Ariakit's [Navigation Menubar](https://ariakit.org/examples/menubar-navigation) a bit more, although it's not perfect either - I wouldn't open the menu on focus and left/right arrow keys should work differently when menu contents are laid out in a grid.

On https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menubar/examples/menubar-navigation/

> The menubar pattern requires complex functionality that is unnecessary for typical site navigation that is styled to look like a menubar with expandable sections or fly outs.

If you don't use `role="menubar"`, those interactions are not strictly necessary

> Radix works slightly better, as when the popup is open, it allows focusing its contents with the DownArrow while our solution focuses the next nav menu item.

This seems correct, will fix

There are a couple of things that can be improved regarding keyboard navigation:
- I'd expect the <kbd>DownArrow</kbd> to open the menu, not to move between triggers (especially if I see down arrows/chevrons). Or, if we decide it shouldn't work like this, it should at least prevent default when the last element is focused (same with <kbd>UpArrow</kbd> and the first item
- <kbd>Space</kbd> should focus the content of the opened menu
- nice to have: we could showcase grid navigation in the Overview menu in the demo (have <kbd>UpArrow</kbd> / <kbd>DownArrow</kbd> navigate between rows)

---

What's the use of the `open` prop on the Root? Wouldn't `value` be enough?

> What's the use of the open prop on the Root? Wouldn't value be enough?

Hmm interesting. A non-nullish value could indeed signal that it's open.

> nice to have: we could showcase grid navigation in the Overview menu in the demo (have UpArrow / DownArrow navigate between rows)

It's a bit hard in the demo only using public APIs due to the fact that it turns into a column on small screens

Focus restoration bug:
1. tab into the first trigger
2. click away to close the menu
3. hover over items
4. when you hover over the Link, the previous Trigger draws the outline


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bfaffc19-25cd-491f-86cb-3770c84ee64f

Testing on my (small) iPhone, the popup closes immediately (pointerdown it seems?) when I try to scroll:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3196278-e23f-4591-ba70-b5c1c403911e

> I see it's reproducible on iPhone by scrolling the content also

Yeah I was trying to touch inside to scroll!

> I see it's reproducible on iPhone by scrolling the content also

Yeah I was trying to touch inside to scroll!
@mj12albert should be fixed - it was caused by the window `resize` causing it to close, I don't think it's strictly necessary except in extreme edge cases
